You politely decline the tea and—

[jitter][red]*SLAM*[/red][/jitter]

The door behind you slams shut. The [blue]tea master[/blue] stares at you for a moment, then presents an ugly cup.
Oh, this tea is for the discourteous...

name: Tezcatara
description:
Oh, a visitor! Do come in, dear!
What can Tezcatara do for you?

Returning.speaker: ancient
Returning.text: Welcome back, sweetie. Let me [jitter][b]burn[/b][/jitter] away your flaws.

Returning.speaker: ancient
Returning.text: Do come in, dear! What can Tezcatara do for you today?

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: Not here to annoy me? What an [jitter][b]unusual[/b][/jitter] little construct...

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: Now how silly would it be if he were unmade by one of his own?

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: Are you sure you want to be fixed up?

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: You [jitter][b]burn[/b][/jitter] so pretty! Come in, come in!

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text:
Such a strong boy you are! Come and eat something.
Can’t [jitter][b]slaughter[/b][/jitter] on an empty stomach!

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text:
Ohh, Architect still alive? Kill him [jitter][b]quickly[/b][/jitter] would you?
I’ll be waiting right here!

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: Now what’s a skeleton doing inside these walls?

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text:
My aim is simple. Vengeance for my family.
I’ll reach the top and make him pay.

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: [jitter][b]Wonderful[/b][/jitter]!

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: Come in, come in! Let’s make your flames burn [jitter][b]brighter[/b][/jitter].

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: Those flames around you remind me of something forbidden.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: What do you know?

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: I know who wrote that [jitter][b]book[/b][/jitter].

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: Oh my, what an [jitter][b]exotic[/b][/jitter] visitor! Who might you be, sweetie?

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I’m no sweetie! Address me with respect, giant... thing-person!

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text:
Where are my manners?
Tezcatara cackles with delight.

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text:
You and your friends are back again?
I’ve made [jitter][b]treats[/b][/jitter] for you!

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: Welcome back regal boy! Looks like you’re doing [jitter][b]great[/b][/jitter] out there.

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: Yes of course! Just some minor setbacks at the upper levels... like dying.

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: We all need to learn from our mistakes.

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: Come closer, quiet one. I can’t see well...

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: You can [jitter][b]trust[/b][/jitter] me.

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: Such a nasty [jitter][b]sickness[/b][/jitter] you have. Let’s take care of you.

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: I’ve met your sisters, you know? Such lovely girls they are.

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: Oh dear... you haven’t been [jitter][b]fighting[/b][/jitter] have you?

First Visit.speaker: ancient
First Visit.text:
Oh, a visitor! Do come in, dear!
What can Tezcatara do for you?

name: The Architect
description: So the arch demon’s thrall has arrived?

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: You’re back? Didn’t I dismantle you?

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: Fix you? Fix your friend? Ridiculous.

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: How do you keep coming back? Is this Neow’s doing?

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: So the arch demon’s thrall has arrived?

Ironclad.speaker: character
Ironclad.text:
Enemy...
Kill...

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: Cursed to fight forever, aren’t you?

Ironclad.speaker: character
Ironclad.text: Architect... arch... enemy!!

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: Do you even know your name?

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: I pity you.

Ironclad.speaker: character
Ironclad.text: Kill...

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: Return to the bottom, Ironclad.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: ARCHITECT! You killed my parents. You killed my friend. You killed my LIFE! ...and now I will kill YOU!

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: Vengeance is it? No thank you.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: ARCHITECT! You killed my parents. You killed my friend. You killed my-

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: You cannot change the past with anger.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: ARCHITECT! You killed my parents. You killed my friend. You killed my LIFE-

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: I did what I must to maintain order.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: ARCHITECT! You killed my parents. You killed my friend. You killed my-

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: Life

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: What...?! Die, monster!

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I’ve arrived! You there, commoner! Is this the top?

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: I need quiet...

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I’ll teach you some manners!

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I’ve arrived again!

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: The loud fool returns?

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I’m neither loud nor a fool! Face me!

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I’ve had enough of your poor manners!

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: Something we can both agree on.

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: Let’s dance!

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: Not even an introduction?

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text:
Where did you come from?
What are you after?

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text:
[jitter]Hrrgh![/jitter]
[jitter][red]DIE!![/red][/jitter]

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text:
Annoying wretch!
[jitter]BE GONE!![/jitter]

name: The Future of Potions?
description:
You feel a faint rumbling as you round the corner to discover a gigantic spinning apparatus!
It has several slots which appear to convert liquids into a highly compressed digestible tablet.

The idea of turning these health tonics into a tiny morsel is uncomfortable but perhaps it's good to try new things?

pages.description: The tablet was disgusting. There is no hope for the future.

You feel a faint rumbling as you round the corner to discover a gigantic spinning apparatus!
It has several slots which appear to convert liquids into a highly compressed digestible tablet.

The idea of turning these health tonics into a tiny morsel is uncomfortable but perhaps it's good to try new things?

name: The Lantern Key
description:
You come upon a faintly glowing key and go to pick it up.

“Been looking all over for that key! Now if you don't mind...”

 This person looks like bad news but maybe it's not good to be judgy.

options.title: RETURN_THE_KEY
options.description: The stranger thanks you for your help, and rewards you handsomely.

pages.description:
You come upon a faintly glowing key and go to pick it up.

“Been looking all over for that key! Now if you don't mind...”

 This person looks like bad news but maybe it's not good to be judgy.
The stranger looks at you menacingly. It seems like only one of you is making it out of here with the key.

name: The Legends Were True
description:
Entering into a pitch dark chamber, a sudden light pierces the darkness from above, just as the door slams behind you.

Illuminated before you appears to be a podium with a map on it. You feel compelled to take it.

Suspicious.

pages.description:
Entering into a pitch dark chamber, a sudden light pierces the darkness from above, just as the door slams behind you.

Illuminated before you appears to be a podium with a map on it. You feel compelled to take it.

Suspicious.
What's the worst that could happen? As you grab the map, an opening appears before you on the other side of the room.

As you sneak around the dark, looking for a way out the room starts to violently shake. With a sudden crash, the entire chamber collapses around you.

Barely making it out of the rubble, you find a vial of something useful that must have been hidden in the room.

name: This or That?
description:
Arms suddenly jut out from a nearby hole, clutching a [gold]suspicious bag of riches[/gold] and a clearly [purple]cursed relic[/purple].

[jitter][blue]“This... or That?”[/blue][/jitter]
a scratched-up voice whispers from below.

options.title: That
options.description: Add [red]Clumsy[/red] to your [gold]Deck[/gold]. Obtain a random [gold]Relic[/gold].

pages.description:
Arms suddenly jut out from a nearby hole, clutching a [gold]suspicious bag of riches[/gold] and a clearly [purple]cursed relic[/purple].

[jitter][blue]“This... or That?”[/blue][/jitter]
a scratched-up voice whispers from below.

options.title: That
options.description: Add [red]Clumsy[/red] to your [gold]Deck[/gold]. Obtain a random [gold]Relic[/gold].

pages.description:
You grab the [purple]Cursed Relic[/purple]. As soon as you grasp it, shackles materialize on your wrists.

You hear a [blue][sine]ghostly laughter[/sine][/blue] and a bright [orange]plume of fire[/orange] fills your vision!

The hole and arms are gone.

You grab the bag and as soon as you do, a [blue][jitter]THIRD hand[/jitter][/blue] appears and clutches at you!!

After a [red][jitter]frantic struggle[/jitter][/red], it releases—the arms and the hole have vanished.

name: Tinker Time
description:
Navigating through an [red][sine]endless sea of corpses[/sine][/red], you find a [orange]mad scientist[/orange] scavenging for various scraps.

“Yes. Hi, hello! You look like a capable fighter... I need a tester for my next [green]atrocity device[/green]! How about it?”

options.title: Accept
options.description: Create a custom card to add to your [gold]Deck[/gold].

pages.description: “What kind of tool will help you [red][jitter]kill everyone[/jitter][/red]?”

“Excellent choice! Now what should it [sine]DO[/sine]?”
The [orange]scientist[/orange] rubs her hands together with glee.

options.title: Choking
options.description: Whenever you play a card this turn, the enemy loses [blue]6[/blue] HP.

options.title: Chaos
options.description: Add a random card into your [gold]Hand[/gold]. It's free to play this turn.

options.title: Improvement
options.description: At the end of combat, [gold]Upgrade[/gold] a random card.

pages.description:
“Whew! All done. This is for you! Now get out there and [red][jitter]slaughter[/jitter][/red]!!”

Little did you know, that [orange]scientist[/orange] went on to create hundreds of weapons, resulting in several thousand deaths within the Spire.

Navigating through an [red][sine]endless sea of corpses[/sine][/red], you find a [orange]mad scientist[/orange] scavenging for various scraps.

“Yes. Hi, hello! You look like a capable fighter... I need a tester for my next [green]atrocity device[/green]! How about it?”

options.title: Accept
options.description: Create a custom card to add to your [gold]Deck[/gold].

name: Trash Heap
description:
You discover a towering pile of [red][jitter]scrapped weapons[/jitter][/red], [orange]discarded trinkets[/orange], and [purple][sine]other oddities[/sine][/purple]. The massive pile shifts and rumbles, as if growing from within...

If you scavenge from the surface, you may find some decent things. But if you really get in there, you may find some [gold]exotic treasures[/gold].

options.title: Dive In
options.description: Lose [red]8[/red] HP. Obtain a random forgotten [gold]Relic[/gold] from the past.

options.title: Grab Random Junk
options.description: Gain [blue]100[/blue] [gold]Gold[/gold]. Obtain a random forgotten card from the past.

pages.description:
You spend a decent amount of time wading through the [red][sine]dangerous garbage[/sine][/red] and find a [gold]relic of the past[/gold]!

Why was this thrown out?

You walk around the pile from a safe distance and pick up some [gold]forgotten valuables[/gold] and [purple][sine]oddities[/sine][/purple] here and there.

Why were these created in the first place?

You discover a towering pile of [red][jitter]scrapped weapons[/jitter][/red], [orange]discarded trinkets[/orange], and [purple][sine]other oddities[/sine][/purple]. The massive pile shifts and rumbles, as if growing from within...

If you scavenge from the surface, you may find some decent things. But if you really get in there, you may find some [gold]exotic treasures[/gold].

options.title: Dive In
options.description: Lose [red]8[/red] HP. Obtain a random forgotten [gold]Relic[/gold] from the past.

options.title: Grab Random Junk
options.description: Gain [blue]100[/blue] [gold]Gold[/gold]. Obtain a random forgotten card from the past.

name: The Trial
description:
You join a line of people entering a massive building.

As you pass under a [gold]golden archway[/gold], horns blare, [jitter]confetti explodes[/jitter], and [sine]streamers glide[/sine] down from the ceiling!

“Entrant [blue][EntrantNumber][/blue], you are the [green]DECIDER[/green] for today's [gold]Trial[/gold].”

pages.description:
You join a line of people entering a massive building.

As you pass under a [gold]golden archway[/gold], horns blare, [jitter]confetti explodes[/jitter], and [sine]streamers glide[/sine] down from the ceiling!

“Entrant [blue][EntrantNumber][/blue], you are the [green]DECIDER[/green] for today's [gold]Trial[/gold].”

A [green]wealthy-looking merchant[/green], presents his case. He is accused of [red]murder[/red] by one of his rivals. The evidence is thin, and you have a feeling that the man is innocent, but the onlookers seem out for blood.

You rule that all of the man's possessions are to be taken and given as processing fees for the court.
The crowd howls in glee!

You go with your conscience and rule that the man is innocent.
Despite the boos and accusations of being a [sine][purple]“paid-off judge”[/purple][/sine] you feel like you did the right thing.

A [gold]powerful noble[/gold] steps forward. You find a note in your pocket indicating that if you decide innocence you will be handsomely rewarded. The [gold]noble[/gold] barely argues against the [purple]corruption charges[/purple] and gives you a knowing smirk.
You can't stop corruption but you can pocket some [gold]gold...[/gold]

The [gold]powerful noble[/gold] was [red]beheaded[/red].
One less [purple]corrupt noble[/purple].

You don't [sine][purple]really[/purple][/sine] know that he's guilty... Yeah, that's it!
The [gold]bribe[/gold] has nothing to do with the outcome here. Nope.

A [orange]nondescript woman with a hard-to-read face[/orange] steps forward. She is charged with the crime of [red]theft[/red] and [purple]con-artistry[/purple]. When the woman speaks, you believe her, but ample witnesses attest to her misdeeds.
Both sides of the story are compelling!

options.title: DECIDE: Guilty
options.description: Add [red]Doubt[/red] to your [gold]Deck[/gold]. Gain [blue]2[/blue] card rewards.

pages.description:
You aren't totally sure, but making the hard choice, you think the evidence is adequate.
Did you really make the right call?

You aren't totally sure, but you have a reasonable doubt.
You don't know if you made the right call, but innocence seems like the better choice.

[jitter]“YOU CANNOT REFUSE!!”[/jitter]

Shouts the [gold]Grand Arbiter of the Courts[/gold].

[jitter]“Those who reject this privilege are penalized with [red]DEATH[/red]. Do you still defy!?”[/jitter]

options.title: Accept
options.description: Give in. Serve as today's Decider.

name: Unrest Site
description:
You find a secluded [gold]Rest Site[/gold] and start a [orange]fire[/orange] to get some rest.
Or so you thought.

Once the fire is started, it begins to swell, reaching not upwards but [sine]sideways[/sine] towards a grove of [purple]oil-seeping trees[/purple].

Should you try resting anyways?

options.title: Kill the Trees
options.description: Lose [red]8[/red] Max HP. Obtain a random [gold]Relic[/gold].

pages.description:
You find a secluded [gold]Rest Site[/gold] and start a [orange]fire[/orange] to get some rest.
Or so you thought.

Once the fire is started, it begins to swell, reaching not upwards but [sine]sideways[/sine] towards a grove of [purple]oil-seeping trees[/purple].

Should you try resting anyways?

options.title: Kill the Trees
options.description: Lose [red]8[/red] Max HP. Obtain a random [gold]Relic[/gold].

pages.description:
These trees are [purple]clearly malicious[/purple] so you decide to get rid of them.
Days later, you're covered in [red]ash[/red] and [purple]oily goop[/purple].

A massive flock of little byrd spirits rise from the slain trees and drop a [gold]small box[/gold] at your feet.
[green]“Thank you for freeing us from those trees... [sine]cheep cheep[/sine]!”[/green]

You should really sleep more.

All this adventuring has left you exhausted, and despite the oddities, you decide to sleep.

You toss and turn as the [orange]fire[/orange] roars and crackles through the night. The [purple]disgusting trees[/purple] give off a wretched scent from their pitch-black sap.

You wake up, recovered, but not replenished.

name: Vakuu
description: Give yourself to me and you will be feared as much as I.

Returning.speaker: ancient
Returning.text: Seeking glory in the Spire are we? I have some propositions for you...

Returning.speaker: ancient
Returning.text: 143. 143 times you’ve been here.

Returning.speaker: ancient
Returning.text: You realize this [sine]never[/sine] ends?

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: Do you find it strange that your fellow constructs want you dead?

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: If you bring me the Architect’s heart, I will restore your [aqua]companion[/aqua].

Defect.speaker: ancient
Defect.text: What’s more important? Your creator or your friend?

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: Imagine seeing [sine]you[/sine] again after so long.

Ironclad.speaker: character
Ironclad.text:
[jitter][b]SACRED DEMON![/b][/jitter]
What have you done to me!?

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: Exactly what you bargained for.

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text:
Do the lost tribes weigh on you...?
Vakuu howls with laughter.

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text: My puppet of puppets... why haven’t you killed the Architect yet?

Ironclad.speaker: character
Ironclad.text: After I kill the Architect, I will kill [jitter][b]YOU![/b][/jitter]

Ironclad.speaker: ancient
Ironclad.text:
[sine]Yawn[/sine]
I look forward to watching you fail.

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: We both seek [sine]vengeance[/sine]. It will have a cost to pay.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: I accept any cost. Every cost for his death.

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: I know where you got that [sine]Hand[/sine].

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: The Architect has gravely wounded us both.

Necrobinder.speaker: character
Necrobinder.text: Get on with it, demon. Show me your contracts.

Necrobinder.speaker: ancient
Necrobinder.text: Straight to business. Very well...

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: A being from the stars? Within these accursed walls?

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: Do you know of me?

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: Yes, I think we can help each other.

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text: More of my power can be yours for a small price.

Regent.speaker: character
Regent.text: I don’t think these deals are good. I’m not feeling great.

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text:
You read the contracts thoroughly have you not?
You have signed them with full intent and responsibility no?

Regent.speaker: ancient
Regent.text:
Are you not going to fulfill your end of the bargain?
How about another contract? Isn’t that better?

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: Killer.

Silent.speaker: character
Silent.text: [sine]...[/sine]

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: This won’t [jitter]end[/jitter] well for you.

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: Vakuu displays his various contracts to the Silent

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: The Architect is not enough, kill your sisters too!

Silent.speaker: character
Silent.text: [sine]...[/sine]

Silent.speaker: ancient
Silent.text: No need for words, we are the same.

First Visit.speaker: ancient
First Visit.text: Give yourself to me and you will be feared as much as I.

name: War Historian, Repy
description:
An academic is trapped in a [purple]hanging cage[/purple] next to a [gold]treasure chest[/gold].

The academic is muttering about [sine][orange]“One-Time-Use Keys”[/orange][/sine] while furiously writing something.

Looks like you can unlock the [purple]cage[/purple] or the [gold]chest[/gold].

options.title: Unlock the Chest
options.description: Lose [gold]Lantern Key[/gold]. Procure [blue]2[/blue] random [gold]Potions[/gold]. Obtain [blue]2[/blue] random [gold]Relics[/gold].

pages.description:
You hear [green]Repy[/green] let out a sigh of relief as you pull out the spare key from your pocket.

“You really had me going there”.
As [green]Repy[/green] walks away, you use your spare key to open the chest.

An academic is trapped in a [purple]hanging cage[/purple] next to a [gold]treasure chest[/gold].

The academic is muttering about [sine][orange]“One-Time-Use Keys”[/orange][/sine] while furiously writing something.

Looks like you can unlock the [purple]cage[/purple] or the [gold]chest[/gold].

options.title: Unlock the Chest
options.description: Lose [gold]Lantern Key[/gold]. Procure [blue]2[/blue] random [gold]Potions[/gold]. Obtain [blue]2[/blue] random [gold]Relics[/gold].

pages.description:
[green]Repy[/green] bows in appreciation as you open the cage. He hands you a [gold]thick leather tome[/gold].

“Those who know not of history are doomed to repeat it. But you seem to know a thing or two about repeats don't you?”

“Not going to free the scholar, [green]Repy[/green]? Based on classic philosophy, you would be considered a [red]moral failure[/red]. That's right, maybe consider the [purple][jitter]LIVING PERSON[/jitter][/purple] that is locked up next time! It is not as if there is a valuable member of society in a [purple][jitter]DIRE PREDICAMENT[/jitter][/purple] or anything!!”

“Look, okay, see how small this cage is!? Why am I complaining? You've already made your unscholarly decision... oh, what's inside there? Some [gold]potions[/gold] and [gold]relics[/gold]? Isn't that just great. [sine]Just great. Really great for you...[/sine]”

[green]Repy's[/green] eyes follow you with disdain as you walk away.

name: Waterlogged Scriptorium
description:
Navigating the murky passages, you stumble upon a [aqua]withered figure[/aqua] working a small shop. The multitude of shelves are [sine]stuffed[/sine] full of damp scrolls and parchment.

Noticing a patron has entered, the [aqua]scribe-shopkeeper[/aqua] snaps to attention and gestures at some [gold]implements[/gold] arranged on a desk.

pages.description:
The [aqua]scribe[/aqua] carefully picks up the vial filled with a [red]bright red ink[/red] and gives it a shake, then gestures with their own hand a dipping motion.

You dip your fingers into the ink and a [green][jitter]jolt of vitality[/jitter][/green] courses through you!

Pleased with your reaction, the [aqua]scribe[/aqua] gives a polite bow and waves you goodbye.

Navigating the murky passages, you stumble upon a [aqua]withered figure[/aqua] working a small shop. The multitude of shelves are [sine]stuffed[/sine] full of damp scrolls and parchment.

Noticing a patron has entered, the [aqua]scribe-shopkeeper[/aqua] snaps to attention and gestures at some [gold]implements[/gold] arranged on a desk.

The [aqua]scribe[/aqua] grabs the [gold]prickly sponge[/gold] (it squeaks) and dabs it across your scrolls.

[jitter][blue]*squeak squeak squeak*[/blue][/jitter]

The prickly texture leaves behind [purple][sine]shimmering marks[/sine][/purple] that seem to stabilize the ink...

The [aqua]scribe[/aqua] lifts the [jitter][gold]wriggly quill[/gold][/jitter] and with swift, practiced movements, etches a strange mark unto your head.

The mark [purple][sine]glows bright[/sine][/purple], then vanishes...

name: Welcome to Wongo's
description:
“Welcome to Wongo's. We have what you want at Wongtastic prices.” says the least enthusiastic clerk you have ever met.

“Peruse our wares and enjoy your time at Wongo's,” they continue, without even looking up.

pages.description:
“We are Wongo-wow'd by your generosity... Wonderful patron, you now have [blue]0[/blue] [gold]Wongo Points[/gold]. With just [blue]0[/blue] more points, you will receive our exclusive [gold]Wongo Customer Appreciation Badge[/gold]. Have a Wongtastic day.”

You have no clue what they are talking about and vow to never return to a Wongo's.

“We are Wongo-wow'd by your generosity... Wonderful patron, you now have [blue]0[/blue] [gold]Wongo Points[/gold]. With just [blue]0[/blue] more points, you will receive our exclusive [gold]Wongo Customer Appreciation Badge[/gold]. Have a Wongtastic day.”

You have already received [blue]0[/blue] of these worthless badges. You vow to never return to a Wongo's.

“We are Wongo-wow'd by your generosity... Wonderful patron, you now have [blue]0[/blue] [gold]Wongo Points[/gold]. Your points have earned you a [gold]Wongo Customer Appreciation Badge[/gold].”
The clerk hands you a cheaply made badge. “Have a Wongtastic day.”

You have now received [blue]0[/blue] badges. You vow to never return to a Wongo's.

“Welcome to Wongo's. We have what you want at Wongtastic prices.” says the least enthusiastic clerk you have ever met.

“Peruse our wares and enjoy your time at Wongo's,” they continue, without even looking up.

“Um... excuse me you can't just leave without buying something at Wongo's,” says the clerk with an irritated tone.

You have a [jitter][red]heated[/red][/jitter] debate over customer choice and it leaves you [sine][purple]mentally drained[/purple][/sine].

name: Wellspring
description:
You trace a [jitter]low rumble[/jitter] to its source and find yourself captivated by a tranquil wellspring. The water is an [sine][aqua]emerald green[/aqua][/sine] with clusters of [sine][gold]glowing motes[/gold][/sine].

It looks inviting. What can possibly go wrong?

options.title: Bathe
options.description: Remove [blue]1[/blue] card from your [gold]Deck[/gold]. Add [blue]1[/blue] [red]Guilty[/red] to your [gold]Deck[/gold].

pages.description:
You emerge from the pool with a slight [red][sine]feeling of unease[/sine][/red].

However, you feel surprisingly refreshed.

Bathing in the [sine][aqua]beautiful water[/aqua][/sine] seems suspicious... you decide to bottle some up instead.

This will come in handy.

name: Whispering Hollow
description:
Making your way through a [red]hollow of dead trees[/red] you happen upon a single bone-white tree. Clay baubles hang off its branches, which curve inward like protective ribs.

Such a [purple]creepy tree[/purple]. It whispers.

[sine][blue]...make exchange.....[/blue][/sine]

options.title: Hug the Tree
options.description: Lose [red]9[/red] HP. Choose a card to [gold]Transform[/gold].

pages.description:
You scatter some [gold]gold[/gold] at the base of the tree and two of the clay vessels drop to the floor soundlessly.

They break open, and contain [green]perfectly intact potions[/green] within.

You nab the potions and flee.

The [blue][sine]whispering[/sine][/blue] compels you to give it [gold]gold[/gold] but you resist and give the tree a big hug instead!

It [red][jitter]thrashes about[/jitter][/red], scratching you with its branches, but eventually calms down. The [green]tree-hugging experience[/green] [gold]transforms[/gold] you.

[purple][sine]...make hug.....?[/sine][/purple]

name: Wood Carvings
description:
You open a dusty box to find a set of 3 elaborate wood carvings:

A bird, snake, and... a torus?

A nearby pedestal has indentations which match the bottom of the carvings. Which carving will you place on the pedestal?

options.title: Locked
options.description: None of your cards can be Enchanted with [purple]Slither[/purple].

options.title: Torus
options.description: Choose [blue]1[/blue] starter card to [gold]Transform[/gold] into [gold]Toric Toughness[/gold].

pages.description:
You place the bird carving on the pedestal and the statue's EYES LIGHT UP and you hear a faint ringing within your mind.

[sine]...caw.... caw caaaawww...[/sine]

The pedestal opens and a strange fluid begins to pour out.
Naturally, you drink it all.

pages.description: You place the snake carving on the pedestal and the statue's SCALES LIGHT UP! You feel a bit warm, comfortable, and wobbly.

You place the torus carving on the pedestal and your STOMACH LIGHTS UP as the statue transforms into a piping hot fried confection. An insatiable hunger takes over and you quickly consume the donut.

You feel tougher, but slower.

name: Zen Weaver
description:
You walk through a spider web and [jitter][red]PANIC[/red][/jitter]!
But then, a [sine][blue]wave of calm[/blue][/sine] washes over you...

[sine]“Calm down. I need you to quit fussing up my web.”[/sine]

How did you calm down so quickly?
An [orange]itsy bitsy[/orange] spider abseils down to meet you. It seems willing to impart some techniques for [gold]gold[/gold].

pages.description:
[sine]“Don't... move...”[/sine] mutters the [orange]miniature spider[/orange] as its forelegs begin to [aqua][jitter]glow[/jitter][/aqua].

Then— in a [jitter]rush of jabbing motions[/jitter], pierces you all over!
A sense of weightlessness comes over you. Well worth the money.

[sine]“Visualize your airways staying open... Each breath is meaningful.”[/sine]

You practice the technique in a way that makes sense to you.

The [orange]teeny-weeny spider[/orange] begins a [sine][purple]hypnotic dance[/purple][/sine] and repeatedly chants.

[sine]“Thoughts don't affect reality. Panic is the thought-killer. Follow the plan.”[/sine]

This makes sense to you. Big wise words from a [orange]tiny spider[/orange].
